skill.name: gitlab-ci-patterns
skill.description: Build GitLab CI/CD pipelines with multi-stage workflows, caching, and distributed runners for scalable automation.
skill.summary: Comprehensive GitLab CI/CD pipeline patterns for automated testing, building, and deployment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 24 potential issues, all determined to be false positives. External command detections are markdown code block delimiters, not actual shell execution. URL references are documentation examples in YAML templates. No cryptographic code exists. This is a safe documentation skill with GitLab CI YAML examples.

content.user_title: Build GitLab CI/CD Pipelines with Multi-Stage Workflows
content.value_statement: Manual GitLab CI pipeline creation is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill provides proven patterns for multi-stage workflows, caching strategies, and deployment automation.
actual_capabilities: Design multi-stage GitLab CI pipelines with build, test, and deploy stages
Configure Docker build and push workflows with proper tagging
Set up multi-environment deployments with staging and production
Implement Terraform validation, planning, and apply workflows
Configure caching strategies for dependencies and build artifacts
Create dynamic child pipelines and trigger configurations
limitations: Only supports GitLab CI/CD, not GitHub Actions or other CI platforms
Requires access to a GitLab instance with appropriate permissions
Cannot execute pipelines directly, only generates configuration
Examples assume Kubernetes and Docker environments

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up New Project
use_cases.description: Quickly establish a complete CI/CD pipeline with testing, building, and deployment stages following GitLab best practices.

use_cases.title: Development Team Migrating to GitLab
use_cases.description: Convert existing CI workflows to GitLab CI format with equivalent functionality and improved caching strategies.

use_cases.title: Platform Engineer Building Templates
use_cases.description: Create reusable pipeline templates and configurations for multiple projects within an organization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pipeline Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline for a Node.js project with build, test, and deploy stages. Include caching for node_modules and artifact collection for the dist folder.

prompt_templates.title: Docker Build Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a GitLab CI job that builds a Docker image, tags it with the commit SHA and latest, and pushes to the GitLab container registry.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Environment Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create GitLab CI jobs for deploying to staging and production environments. Staging should deploy automatically on develop branch, production requires manual approval on main branch.

prompt_templates.title: Terraform Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete Terraform pipeline with validation, planning, and apply stages. Include proper artifact handling for plans and manual approval for production changes.

output_examples.input: Create a basic GitLab CI pipeline for a Python project
output_examples.output: Generates a .gitlab-ci.yml with stages for linting, testing, and building, including pytest configuration, coverage reporting, and artifact collection.

output_examples.input: Set up Kubernetes deployment from GitLab CI
output_examples.output: Produces deployment jobs with kubectl configuration, environment definitions, rollout status checks, and proper namespace targeting for staging and production.

best_practices: Use specific image tags instead of latest to ensure reproducible builds
Cache dependencies appropriately to reduce pipeline execution time
Implement manual approval gates for production deployments
anti_patterns: Storing secrets directly in .gitlab-ci.yml instead of using CI/CD variables
Using overly broad artifact paths that bloat pipeline storage
Skipping test stages to speed up pipelines, compromising code quality

faq.question: How do I securely store API keys and credentials in GitLab CI?
faq.answer: Use GitLab CI/CD Variables in your project settings. Mark sensitive variables as protected and masked. Reference them in your pipeline using $VARIABLE_NAME syntax.

faq.question: What is the difference between cache and artifacts in GitLab CI?
faq.answer: Cache speeds up jobs by reusing dependencies across pipelines. Artifacts pass build outputs between jobs within the same pipeline. Use cache for dependencies, artifacts for build outputs.

faq.question: How do I trigger a pipeline only on specific branches?
faq.answer: Use the rules keyword or the older only/except keywords. Rules provide more flexibility with conditions like if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main".

faq.question: Can I run multiple jobs in parallel in GitLab CI?
faq.answer: Yes, jobs in the same stage run in parallel. Define multiple jobs under the same stage to execute them concurrently, limited by your Runner availability.

faq.question: How do I create a pipeline that runs on a schedule?
faq.answer: Configure Pipeline Schedules in GitLab project settings under CI/CD. Set the cron expression and target branch. Use $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE to detect scheduled runs.

faq.question: What are GitLab CI templates and how do I use them?
faq.answer: Templates provide pre-configured jobs for common tasks like SAST scanning or dependency checking. Include them using the include keyword with template references.
